channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This travel site is the Google Maps helper you never knew you needed
item.description: Trying to get from point A to point B? If only it were that simple! With any manner of travel these days, you’ve got options: planes, trains, buses, ferries, and beyond. And finding the best path to embark on isn’t always easy. Even finding all the available options can sometimes be a pain.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hoffman, Fast Company

item.title: This music streamer is giving its users a version of Spotify Wrapped every month
item.description: With music streaming, users have gotten used to being at the mercy of algorithms. But French music streamer Deezer is making it easier for its subscribers to make the algorithm work for them.
item.dc:creator: Lana Tleimat, Fast Company

item.title: Fresh iPhone 17 dummy unit leak may show camera bumps and MagSafe connectors for all four models
item.description: We've got another look at the size and the shape of the four models Apple is expected to unveil this year.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stop sorting your garbage with this new technology
item.description: Recycling robots are using artificial intelligence to learn how to sort recyclables by recognizing patterns in colors, textures, shapes and logos.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Google previews Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model
item.description: Google has introduced an early preview of its Gemini 2.5 Flash hybrid reasoning model. An early version of the model is available through the Gemini API via Google AI Studio and Vertex AI, according to an April 17 Google blog post.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Caltrans using remote-controlled equipment to help clear Highway 1 slide near Big Sur
item.description: Regent’s Slide — post mile 27.8 — occurred March 9, 2024, and started seeing top-down removal of slide material by crews on April 30, 2024. The slide originated 450 feet above the roadway displacing material that engulfed Highway 1 and continuing down to the beach and ocean below.
item.dc:creator: James Herrera, The Mercury News

item.title: Roblox, Discord scrutinized by states over child safety issues
item.description: Roblox, which has 85 million people play games on its site daily, has been criticized for not doing enough to protect its young user base.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok is obsessed with this investor who bought 30 floors of a Chicago skyscraper
item.description: One of the more unique takes on the POV trend on TikTok: “POV: You bought a 100-year-old skyscraper . . .
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Google "could face breakup" after being found guilty of having illegal ad tech monopolies
item.description: Google has been found guilty of antitrust law violation with regards to its ads market monopolization – and the consequences could be major.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft 365: A guide to the updates
item.description: Microsoft 365 (and Office 365) subscribers get more frequent software updates than those who have purchased Office without a subscription, which means subscribers have access to the latest features, security patches, and bug fixes.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple and Google eye the future of AI glasses
item.description: Apple and Google know that smart glasses will replace the smartphone as the main device people use the most, according to recent news. And the two leading smartphone platform makers don’t want Meta to own the future of mobile computing, but they know Meta is leading the race so far.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: British businesses are getting used to AI at work - but there are still plenty of hurdles to overcome
item.description: British businesses are benefiting from AI, but the same old hurdles still prevent some from getting the full benefits.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The US has a single rare earths mine. Chinese export limits are energizing a push for more
item.description: America’s only rare earths mine has fielded calls from anxious companies since China responded to President Donald Trump's tariffs by limiting exports of seven heavy metals.
item.dc:creator: Josh Funk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Facebook sending out $40 payments to users over 'Like button' issue
item.description: The lawsuit claimed Facebook was using cookies to collect data on users in 2010 and 2011, even when they were logged out or visiting other websites.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Nvidia watches its Trump overtures come to naught
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Netflix's first quarter builds on recent momentum as trade war drags down other tech companies
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Netflix fared better than analysts anticipated during the first three months of the year, signaling the world’s largest video streaming service is still thriving as President Donald Trump’s policies cast a pall on the economy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why was Zoom knocked offline this week? There’s some finger-pointing
item.description: There’s a lot of finger-pointing going on between providers trying to explain this week’s two-hour outage of Zoom’s American service. On its website, Zoom says its zoom.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Lawmakers launch investigation into 23andMe after bankruptcy
item.description: A group of House lawmakers launched a probe Thursday into 23andMe's handling of customers' sensitive data following the company's filing for bankruptcy last month. In a letter to 23andMe, House Commerce and Energy Committee Chair Brett Guthrie (R-Ky.) and Reps. Gus Bilirakis (R-Fla.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Broadcom (quietly) brings back free VMware ESXi hypervisor
item.description: With little notice or fanfare, Broadcom is offering a free version of the VMware ESXi hypervisor for download. VMware vSphere Hypervisor version 8 can be downloaded from Broadcom’s support portal for free, but registration is required.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: You need to update your Apple devices immediately
item.description: Things are getting serious regarding Apple security. This shouldn’t really surprise you, given the normalization of chaos we’re enduring, as confusion always makes people more vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Insta360 teaser suggests it could launch world's best 360 camera soon – here are 5 things I want to see
item.description: Insta360 has posted a teaser for a new launch event on April 22, and rumors are pointing towards a new X5. Here's what we want to see from the 360 camera.
item.dc:creator: mark.wilson@futurenet.com (Mark Wilson), TechRadar

item.title: Google's digital ad network declared an illegal monopoly, joining its search engine in penalty box
item.description: Google has been branded an abusive monopolist by a federal judge for the second time in less than a year, this time for illegally exploiting some of its online marketing technology to boost the profits fueling an internet empire currently worth $1.8 trillion.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New Marvel trailer for The Fantastic Four: First Steps reveals first looks at Reed Richards using his stretchy powers, The Silver Surfer, and other Galactus-sized teases
item.description: The Fantastic Four: First Steps' official trailer has been released publicly following its CinemaCon 2025 debut.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Many Americans share mistrust of artificial intelligence: Survey
item.description: Three-quarters of Americans say they trust information generated by artificial intelligence (AI) only sometimes or hardly ever, according to a recent survey.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: GE Vernova’s CEO on thriving through tariffs and supply chain shifts
item.description: Amid tariff whiplash and the rejuggling of global trade, GE Vernova’s CEO Scott Strazik is finding a way to stay “relentlessly optimistic.
item.dc:creator: Robert Safian, Fast Company

item.title: Trump's potential semiconductor tariffs spook tech industry
item.description: President Trump’s potential tariffs on semiconductors are stoking alarm within the technology sector as companies brace for ripple effects across the industry and its competitive standing on a global stage.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Gleam 1.10 improves compiler, JavaScript codegen
item.description: Gleam 1.10, a new release of the type-safe programming language for the Erlang virtual machine and JavaScript runtimes, is now available. The update features compiler improvements centering on holding more information and improving exhaustive analysis.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg wraps up testimony in antitrust case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg emphasized in federal court on Wednesday that he bought Instagram and WhatsApp because he saw value in the companies — not to take out competitors, as the Federal Trade Commission alleges in a historic antitrust trial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nvidia unveils $5.5B charge to comply with Trump rule
item.description: Nvidia revealed Tuesday the Trump administration's tighter export controls on computer chips will cost the company $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Tech shares fall after Nvidia says new US controls on exports of AI chip will cost it $5.5 billion
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Shares in computer chipmakers slumped Wednesday after Nvidia said tighter U.S. government controls on exports of computer chips used for artificial intelligence will cost it an extra $5.5 billion.
item.dc:creator: ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 6 languages you can deploy to WebAssembly right now
item.description: WebAssembly, or Wasm, gives developers a way to create programs that run at near-native speed in the browser or anywhere else you can deploy the WebAssembly runtime. But you generally don’t write programs in Wasm directly.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Don't like a columnist's opinion? Los Angeles Times offers an AI-generated opposing viewpoint
item.description: In a colorful commentary for the Los Angeles Times, Matt K. Lewis argued that callousness is a central feature of the second Trump administration, particularly its policies of deportation and bureaucratic cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Meta CEO Zuckerberg considered spinning off Instagram in 2018 over antitrust worries, email says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg once considered separating Instagram from its parent company due to worries about antitrust litigation, according to an email shown Tuesday on the second day of an antitrust trial alleging Meta illegally monopolized the social media market.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: China pursuing 3 alleged US operatives over cyberattacks during Asian Games
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — China said Tuesday it is pursuing three alleged U.S. operatives accused of carrying out cyberattacks on Chinese infrastructure during the Asian Games held in the city of Harbin in February. A notice from the Harbin police headquarters named them as Katheryn A. Wilson, Robert J.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTOPHER BODEEN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Measuring success in dataops, data governance, and data security
item.description: Back in 2006, British mathematician Clive Humby stated that data was the new oil. Like oil, data isn’t useful in its raw state and must be refined, processed, and distributed to deliver value.
item.dc:creator: Info World
